Amazon Now brings ultra-fast delivery to key markets like Mexico and Brazil. Shoppers can now get groceries, perishables, and household essentials delivered in just 15-30 minutes. To make this happen, $AMZN is teaming up with local gig-economy delivery apps like Rappi. They are also utilizing small micro-fulfillment centers located in dense urban areas. This helps Amazon bypass traditional infrastructure bottlenecks and instantly beat standard shipping speeds.

$MELI is doubling down on its huge proprietary logistics network. They already slashed the free shipping minimums so customers can afford to buy more cheap items every single day. MELI is also pouring billions of dollars into new mega warehouses and dedicated cargo planes to increase delivery density. Their ultimate strategy connects this unbeatable shipping scale with the Mercado Pago financial ecosystem to completely lock in customer loyalty. They are also steadily increasing their ecosystem value with MELI+ offering nearly unbeatable entertainment bundles. MELI is happily taking a short-term hit to its profit margins to win this war and defend its market share. The battle for e-commerce dominance in Latin America continues on bet MELI’s approach imo is more focused on sustainable longterm growth.

El argumento es válido porque Cuba es un ejemplo concreto de un país que cambió a un modo de producción no capitalista (propiedad estatal mayoritaria, sin mercado libre pleno). En la práctica: crisis actual con escasez, apagones y +1M de emigrantes desde 2021 huyendo a países capitalistas como México o EE.UU. por mejores oportunidades. PIB per cápita Cuba ~7,500 USD vs México ~14,000 USD.

Ejemplo simple/meme: como criticar "el sistema" pero vivir de sus frutos y no probar el "otro" real. Es un reto de coherencia: ve y compara resultados.

Poco a poco: ayuda a reflexionar si la crítica es al capitalismo o a fallos corregibles, vs sistemas que en teoría suenan bien pero en datos muestran límites.
